behavior: Active, quick-moving foliage forager; frequently flicks or cocks its tail while gleaning insects and spiders.
field_marks: Tiny, slim songbird with a long-legged, delicate profile
Pale blue-gray upperparts and grayish-white underparts
Long mostly black tail with white outer feathers or edges; often held cocked
Breeding male may show a narrow black V above the eyes
habitat: Broadleaf and mixed woodlands, woodland edges, thickets, and scrub; often favors moist areas or vegetation near water.
